title: geum × cortlandicum
intro: Geum × cortlandicum, also known as the round-leaved avens, is a hybrid perennial plant in the family Rosaceae, originating from the intersection of Geum urbanum and Geum rivale. The medicinal properties of Geum × cortlandicum lie in its roots and leaves, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, and have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Geum × cortlandicum include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health issues. Geum × cortlandicum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which can be used to address various health concerns.
